title: Baby Misty
summary: CIRI shareholder Kathy King is proud to announce the birth of her second granddaughter, Misty Mildred Kay Waskey. Misty was born Jan. 1 at 6:36 a.m. and weighed 7 pounds, 10 ounces. title: CIRI Spotlight:  Chrystal Moon
summary: CIRI shareholder Chrystal Marie Moon is the only health aide in her village of Tyonek, Alaska, and that means high stress and being on-call virtually any moment of the day for a minor health concern or a serious injury or illness. title: Master of Arts in Humanities
summary: CIRI shareholder Ruth Ann Oskolkoff graduated Dec. 31, 2008, with a Master of Arts in Humanities from California State University, Dominguez Hills. title: Master of Public Administration
summary: CIRI shareholder Michael L. Heyward earned his Master of Public Administration from the University of Alaska Anchorage in December 2008. title: Baby Shyann
summary: CIRI descendant Shyann Kennedy Bartley was born Oct. 29, 2008, to proud parents Benjamin Bartley and CIRI shareholder Saundra Noble. Shyann weighed 10 pounds, 12 ounces. title: “Pick-Click-Give” includes CIRI nonprofits
summary: Alaskans considering donating some or all of their Permanent Fund dividend to the PFD Charitable Contributions Program, known as “Pick-Click-Give,” can find organizations in the CIRI family and other Alaska Native entities in the program’s list of charitable groups. title: CITC to produce Cook Inlet region services directory
summary: Cook Inlet Tribal Council (CITC) assumed the production of The Services Directory for Alaska Native and Other Native American Families, a community directory that has been published by The CIRI Foundation (TCF) since 1999. title: 2008 CIRI shareholder tax information
summary: CIRI has mailed IRS 1099 forms to all shareholders for the 2008 tax year. Some shareholders will receive a second 1099 form if they also received 7(j) payments and/or shareholder prizes. title: Patrick wins January Stock Will prize
summary: Donald Lee Patrick of New York won the January 2009 Stock Will participation prize. title: Shareholder question
summary: I am turning 65 soon. What do I need to do to make sure I begin to receive Elder payments? No action needs to be taken by Elders becoming eligible to receive payments from CIRI’s Irrevocable Elders’ Settlement Trust. title: A word from the president:  Challenging economy is cause to be solemn, not paralyzed
summary: By Margie Brown, CIRI president and CEO Alaska has been weathering the national recession better than most of the country because our state’s economy is based on a resilient mix of oil and natural gas development, U.S. military presence, tourism and income from other natural resources. Consequently, Alaska real estate markets are steady, relatively few Alaskans have lost their jobs and business confidence is generally positive. title: Former director inducted into Women of Achievement
summary: BP Exploration (Alaska) Inc. and YWCA Anchorage selected CIRI shareholder and former Board member Agnes Brown as one of its Women of Achievement at the program’s 2008 awards luncheon on Dec. 10, 2008. title: CIRI executive team sets tone for corporate citizenship
summary: CIRI believes its long-term prospects are intertwined with the well being of the communities not only in its region, but everywhere it has a presence or does business. title: Sonterra hotel construction underway
summary: Workers are building the new 126-room Hyatt Place hotel at CIRI Land Development Co.’s (CLDC) Sonterra office park project in San Antonio, Texas.
